author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Donatist Controversy.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

ccel.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

title: Chapter 40

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "Paul the apostle also bids us, ‘Be ye not unequally yoked with unbelievers: for what fellowship hath righteousness with unrighteousness? and what communion hath light with darkness? and what concord hath Christ with Belial? or what part hath he that believeth with an infidel?’"

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: I recognize the words of the apostle; but how they can help you I cannot see at all.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For which of us says that there is any fellowship between righteousness and unrighteousness, even though the righteous and the unrighteous, as in the case of Judas and Peter, should be alike partakers of the sacraments?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For from one and the same holy thing Judas received judgment to himself and Peter salvation, just as you received the sacrament with Optatus, and, if you were unlike him, were not therefore partakers in his robberies.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Who would be mad enough to assert that?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: What fellowship was there, then, on the part of your righteousness with his unrighteousness, when you approached together to the same altar?
